ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly states 'Yakon'. Depicted figure is a hulking green alien creature with spiky protrusions, large clawed hands, and a wide mouth — matching Yakon's canonical appearance as a giant insectoid/reptilian alien from the Planet of Darkness. Card special traits Alien/World Tournament and era The Evil Wizard Babidi Saga confirm identity.

trivia_bullets.text: Razor's Edge Yakon (TB2-017) is a Common Battle card from the World Martial Arts Tournament themed booster set (2018), indexed to The Evil Wizard Babidi Saga and carrying the special traits Alien/World Tournament. Yakon is one of Babidi's three primary henchmen (alongside Pui Pui and Dabura), deployed to drain energy for Majin Buu's resurrection [dbs-cardgame.com: TB02 card list].
sources: WebSearch snippet: Razor's Edge Yakon TCG details

trivia_bullets.text: TB2-017's game mechanic accumulates cards under itself (placed from the top of your deck when your opponent attacks), gaining +5000 power per card stacked beneath it — a 'growth under pressure' effect. The card self-destructs when 5 or more cards are placed under it. This mirrors Yakon's in-story ability: he can absorb light energy to grow stronger, but overloads and explodes when Goku feeds him too much by transforming into a Super Saiyan [WebSearch snippet: Razor's Edge Yakon card details; Dragon Ball Wiki: Yakon].
sources: WebSearch snippet: Razor's Edge Yakon card details
dragon-ball-official.com: Weekly Character Showcase Yakon no. 163

trivia_bullets.text: Yakon originates from the Planet of Darkness, a world permanently shrouded in shadow. His species — Anoku-seijin — evolved to see in the dark and absorb light as sustenance. Goku defeats him not through superior combat, but by overfeeding his light-absorbing ability: going Super Saiyan produces so much light energy that Yakon literally explodes from overload. This makes Yakon one of the few Dragon Ball Z antagonists defeated by the mechanic of his own power rather than being outmatched in a fight [dragon-ball-official.com: Weekly Character Showcase Yakon no. 163; Dragon Ball Wiki: Yakon via WebSearch snippet].
sources: dragon-ball-official.com: Weekly Character Showcase Yakon no. 163
WebSearch snippet: Dragon Ball Wiki Yakon
